turns: Production is throwing 500s on POST /api/orders since 14:23 UTC. Logs show 'TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading items)' in createOrder handler. Last deploy was 14:18 UTC. What do I check first?

Rolled back to previous deploy and 500s stopped. Now I need to find what changed. The PR that shipped at 14:18 was a refactor of cart serialization. Here's the diff for the suspected file: function buildCart(cart) { return { id: cart.id, total: cart.items.reduce((s, i) => s + i.price * i.qty, 0) }; }. Original was: function buildCart(cart) { return { id: cart.id, items: cart.items, total: cart.items.reduce((s, i) => s + i.price * i.qty, 0) }; }. What broke?

Right, items field was dropped. Downstream order service expects items. Fix: re-add items in buildCart. But I want to add a regression test so this can't happen again. What's the right test shape? We use Jest.

Test added and passes. Now reviewing the rest of that PR. There's also a change to the inventory check: it now uses Promise.all over an array of SKU lookups instead of a sequential loop. Is that safe under our DB connection pool of 20 max?
OK so the parallel call could exhaust the pool if a single order has many SKUs. Average order has 3 SKUs, max we've seen is 47. Recommend a fix that's safer but doesn't slow down the common case.

I want to add a 'gift card' feature to our checkout. User enters a code at checkout, code applies a balance, balance is consumed up to its remaining value, leftover charged to card. What are the main design decisions I need to make?

Good list. Let's start with: should gift card balances be tracked as immutable transaction events (event-sourced) or as a mutable balance row that we update? We already use mutable rows for user wallets. Pros and cons of each for this case.

Mutable row it is, for consistency with wallets. Next: race conditions. Two concurrent checkouts could both read the same balance and try to consume it. How do we prevent double-spending the same gift card?

PostgreSQL row-level lock with SELECT FOR UPDATE inside the checkout transaction. Got it. Now: gift cards expire 12 months after issue. Where does the expiry check go - in the consumption code path, in a daily batch job, or both?
Both makes sense. One last thing: refunds. If the user gets a partial refund on an order that used a gift card + credit card, where does the refund go? Walk me through the policy and the code path.
